assertions.description: The opening paragraph names the project, the subject, and the public-interest case within the first three sentences
The proposal uses at least one phrase or framing directly drawn from the funder's stated priorities
The closing paragraph clearly states what the grant will make possible — specific deliverables or actions, not just a restatement of the project
Output contains all required sections: Title/Tagline/Specs, Film Description (2 paragraphs), Festival & Awards (if provided), Credits, and Contact
The tagline is one sentence that captures the film's emotional or thematic core — distinct from the logline
Film description is specific enough to distinguish this film from any other documentary on a similar subject
Output contains all required sections: Project Overview, Why This Territory, Co-Production Proposal (with bullet-pointed terms), Financing Structure, International Potential, Next Steps
The co-production offer is specific: percentage, rights bundle, editorial involvement, and credit are stated
The next step is concrete — a specific meeting, call, or market event — not generic interest language
Output contains Logline, Editorial Hook (why now), The Story, Approach, Comparable Titles, Production Status, and Contact/Call to Action sections
The logline is one sentence containing the subject, the angle, and a sense of stakes
Output is 400-600 words total and written in third person with no scene-by-scene detail — sells concept and team, not sequences
